title: Using an upward looking camera to calculate tool offsets
description:
Using an upward looking camera to calculate tool offsets

Using a fixed upward camera to calculate tool offsets

Here is an example of how to use a fixed upward camera to calculate tool offsets.   It uses the RobotToolXYU result to determine the tool offsets. The function first runs a sequence to locate the tip of the tool.  Then the tool offsets are retrieved using VGet RobotToolXYU.

Function DefineTool

  Integer found

  Real xTool, yTool, uTool

  VRun findTip

  VGet findTip.tip.RobotToolXYU, found, xTool, yTool, uTool

  If found Then

    TLSet 1, XY(xTool, yTool, 0, 0)

  EndIf

Fend
examples:
Function DefineTool Integer found Real xTool, yTool, uTool VRun findTip VGet findTip.tip.RobotToolXYU, found, xTool, yTool, uTool If found Then
    TLSet 1, XY(xTool, yTool, 0, 0) EndIf
Fend
